name: paypal-transaction
description: Reads PayPal transactions, balances, catalog products, and customer disputes through the PayPal REST API using OAuth 2.0 client-credentials auth.

risk.read: external PayPal REST API read of transaction, balance, catalog, and dispute data
risk.approval: none; read-only, no reverse-ETL write surface

conformance.reason: custom-auth-only (mode:custom AuthHook exchanging client_id/client_secret for a Bearer token via HTTP Basic client authentication at the OAuth2 token endpoint) AND the disputes stream is StreamHook-handled (PayPal's HATEOAS links[{rel,href}] array pagination shape, see docs.md's Streams notes); conformance's synthetic non-secret config sets base_url to the literal string "synthetic-conformance-value", never the replay server's own address (withReplayURL only overrides the bundle's HTTP.URL field used for stream/check request resolution, not RuntimeConfig.Config["base_url"] the AuthHook itself reads to derive token_url), so the AuthHook's real HTTP POST can never land on the replay server -- every auth-resolving dynamic check would otherwise fail identically and uninformatively trying to dial an invalid host. Hook-covered, proven live by internal/connectors/paritytest/paypal-transaction (token exchange + disputes two-page HATEOAS pagination against a real httptest.Server) and internal/connectors/hooks/paypal-transaction/hooks_test.go, plus the declarative pagination/records/schema shape for transactions/balances/products, which every OTHER goldens/pilot bundle's own conformance run already proves generically for page_number/offset/none-pagination streams.
